reviews.review_title: If you love archery, you’ll love it!
reviews.review_description: So far just starting out but I love it so far.  Challenging but very easy to follow and good exercise for the arms.  Senior approved!!

Just discovered this game and already having a blast. Feel like more people need to know about it. The bow and arrow dynamic is a blast and super satisfying, as is dodging enemy arrows. Overall the feel and polish is great and loving the vibey electronic music. Also fwiw I get motion sickness in a bunch of games but not feeling it at all in this one.

EDIT:  I actually purchased the game again because I could not stop thinking about it (which is a bit weird if you ask me). I hope that when the game does come out of early access it will have a lot more levels as well as a one-on-one duel mode and even a co-op mode. Have added an additional star to my initial review because of this. 


Like the title says: a really fun game but for some reason the bow is a bit too high for me and drawing my arm back, the headset gets in the way. Would really help if there was a hight adjustment setting. Also would like to suggest some kind of multiplayer duel option if possible.

Fun game!! Worth the 10 bucks. I’m unsure how long is the campaign but it’s a blast. Very reminiscent of Superhot but more surprise enemies and not able to control time. If you like using a bow you’ll like this game!

reviews.review_title: The most basic handle positioning issue
reviews.review_description:
The gameplay is really good, and I love the feature where you can use arrows to cancel out incoming enemy attacks! I hope future updates will include more levels.

However, the basic hand positioning for holding and drawing the bow is too low and doesn’t align with the controller handles. The arrows shoot slightly higher than expected, so I have to deliberately aim lower to hit accurately.

It would be great if the basic bow handling could reference Arrowhead - Medieval Archery VR. It would be even better if there were options to adjust this, as it’s the fundamental mechanic in a bow-focused game. If this could be improved, I would highly recommend it to others!
